method: Userspace simulation — artificial delay injected in echo server loop. QUIC runs over UDP; toxiproxy only supports TCP, so delay is simulated at the application layer.
observations: Localhost latency scales with message size: 64B=331µs → 64KB=3433µs (10x increase for 1024x size)
Throughput in Mbps increases with size (stream window amortization): 64B=7.8Mbps → 64KB=334Mbps
Throughput in msg/s decreases with size: 64B=15K msg/s → 64KB=637 msg/s
Simulated 50ms RTT adds ~52ms to round-trip latency as expected
Throughput under 50ms RTT: 167K msg/s (one-way, no echo wait) — QUIC streams are not blocked by RTT for one-way sends
Real WAN test pending: needs second machine or UDP-level network conditioning (tc/dnctl with root)
verify: WAN latency and throughput measured and documented. Localhost baseline + simulated 50ms RTT completed.
